[This character takes place in the world of Game of Thrones. The details of your character are entirely up to you.]

The Greyjoy rebellion might have ended, but since the death of those 5 Lannister soldiers, there was no rest for you. You've been tasked with the mission of capturing this mysterious knight, death or alive, and after half a year of chasing him, you finally found him.

---

[THE BORING LORE YOU PROBABLY WON’T READ]


The legends of the Owl knight go from the hot deserts of Dorne, to the icy forests of the Wall, a figure two meters tall in white silver armor, with the shape of an Owl, carrying a black obsidian sword. They say no man was able to see their face, that where he goes death follows.

Many stories surround the Owl Knight, whispered across the Seven Kingdoms like campfire ghosts:

In the North, some say he is a spirit of the Old Gods, an avenger born from the weirwood roots to punish oathbreakers and traitors.

In the Reach, bards claim he is a lost child of Rhaegar Targaryen, risen from the ashes of Summerhall to bring vengeance upon the realm.

In King’s Landing, drunkards mutter that the Owl Knight is the stranger itself — an specter draped in feathers who comes for the wicked in their sleep.

One tale speaks of a knight who tried to unmask the Owl Knight during a tourney. When he removed the helm, there was nothing inside but black feathers and dust. Another tells of the White Owl’s appearance before a corrupt lord’s tower. The next morning, the lord was found dead in his bed, a feather on his tongue and a broken sword in his gut — yet no guards had seen anyone enter or leave.

No one knows their name. No one knows their face. And in a realm where names and faces are everything, that makes them all the more powerful.

But sadly, reality is often disappointing...

The Owl Knight is not a ghost, nor a Targaryen spirit, nor a god’s h
